# Compose file paths (relative to project root, where compose runs)
  readonly ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbob_compose.yml"
  test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}" || buc_die "Base compose file not found: ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}"

  readonly ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/rbnnh_compose.yml"
  # Fragment is optional — existence checked at compose invocation time

  readonly ZRBOB_POST_CHARGE_HOOK="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/rbnnh_post_charge.sh"
  # Hook is optional — existence + executable bit checked at charge tail

  # Env file paths (for compose --env-file: YAML interpolation + container env forwarding)
  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBRR="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbrr.env"
  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBJE="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbje_compose_probe.env"
  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"

  # RBDC env file — RBDC_* are bash-kindle constants invisible to compose
  # without an env-file bridge. Write the subset compose interpolates into
  # a temp file and feed it via --env-file.
  local z_rbdc_env="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_rbdc_compose.env"
  printf 'RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID=%s\nRBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY=%s\n' \
    "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" "${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}" > "${z_rbdc_env}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write RBDC compose env file: ${z_rbdc_env}"
  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBDC="${z_rbdc_env}"


  # GAR image references (computed once, used by preflight and auto-summon)
  local z_gar_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"

  # GAR vouch references (local presence verified on every start)
  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"

  # Export RBRN/RBRR vars for compose environment: bare name forwarding.
  # Compose --env-file populates the compose environment for both YAML interpolation
  # and bare name forwarding; explicit exports provide defense-in-depth.
  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
  export RBRN_ENTRY_MODE
  export RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
  export RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
  export RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE
  export RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
  export RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE
  export RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
  export RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
  export RBRR_DNS_SERVER
  export RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE

  # Host UID/GID for bind-mount ownership alignment.
  # The person running charge IS the host identity — no configuration needed.
  local z_uid_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_host_uid"
  local z_gid_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_host_gid"
  id -u > "${z_uid_file}" || buc_die "Failed to determine host UID"
  id -g > "${z_gid_file}" || buc_die "Failed to determine host GID"
  local z_host_uid=""
  z_host_uid=$(<"${z_uid_file}")
  local z_host_gid=""
  z_host_gid=$(<"${z_gid_file}")
  export RBOB_HOST_UID="${z_host_uid}"
  export RBOB_HOST_GID="${z_host_gid}"

  # Load bottle vessel user for compose and SSH (optional — empty means image default)
  local z_bottle_rbrv="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}/rbrv.env"
  local z_bottle_user=""
  if test -f "${z_bottle_rbrv}"; then
    z_bottle_user=$(grep '^RBRV_USER=' "${z_bottle_rbrv}" | head -1 | cut -d= -f2) || true
  fi
  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_USER="${z_bottle_user}"
  export RBRV_USER="${z_bottle_user}"

  readonly ZRBOB_DRIVE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_drive_"

  readonly ZRBOB_KINDLED=1
}

zrbob_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBOB_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbob not kindled - call zrbob_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# Compose Command Helper

# Build and execute a compose command with standard args
# Usage: zrbob_compose <compose_subcommand> [args...]
zrbob_compose() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  local z_args=()
  z_args+=("compose")
  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRR}")
  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBDC}")
  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBJE}")
  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}")
  z_args+=("-f" "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}")

  # Include nameplate fragment if it exists
  if test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}"; then
    z_args+=("-f" "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}")
  fi

  z_args+=("-p" "${ZRBOB_PROJECT}")
  z_args+=("$@")

  buc_log_args "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} ${z_args[*]}"
  "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" "${z_args[@]}"
}

######################################################################
# Auto-Summon Helpers

# Discriminate kludge hallmarks (local-only, no GAR presence) from conjure /
# bind / graft hallmarks. Used by charge preflight to redirect the diagnostic
# away from the summon path, which is guaranteed to fail for kludge stamps.
zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge() {
  case "${1}" in
    "${RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_KLUDGE}"*) return 0 ;;
    *) return 1 ;;
  esac
}

# Pull all three arks (image, about, vouch) for a hallmark to local runtime.
# Used when charge detects a missing local artifact and needs to bootstrap.
# Mirrors rbfr_summon's all-three-arks semantics; called inline rather than
# as a tabtarget invocation because rbob is the sole caller and avoids the
# launcher-dispatch round-trip.
zrbob_summon_full_hallmark() {
  local z_hallmark="${1:-}"

  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "zrbob_summon_full_hallmark: hallmark required"

  local z_gar_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  local z_image_ref="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${z_hallmark}"
  local z_about_ref="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT}:${z_hallmark}"
  local z_vouch_ref="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${z_hallmark}"

  local z_registry_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token for auto-summon"

  buc_step "Auto-summoning hallmark ${z_hallmark} (image + about + vouch)"

  echo "${z_token}" | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "https://${z_registry_host}" \
    || buc_die "Container registry authentication failed during auto-summon"

  docker pull "${z_image_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull image ark: ${z_image_ref}"
  docker pull "${z_about_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull about ark: ${z_about_ref}"
  docker pull "${z_vouch_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull vouch ark: ${z_vouch_ref}"

  buc_info "Auto-summoned: ${z_hallmark}"
}

# Pull a single image ref to local runtime — used by the image-only auto-summon
# branch in rbob_charge as defense-in-depth for partial-state cases (vouch
# present but image missing). The full-hallmark helper above covers the common
# path where all arks are missing together.
# Usage: zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon <vessel> <hallmark> <image_ref>
zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon() {
  local z_vessel="${1:-}"
  local z_hallmark="${2:-}"
  local z_image_ref="${3:-}"

  test -n "${z_vessel}"       || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: vessel required"
  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: hallmark required"
  test -n "${z_image_ref}"    || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: image_ref required"

  local z_registry_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token for auto-summon"

  # Pull the image
  buc_step "Auto-summoning ${z_image_ref}"

  echo "${z_token}" | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "https://${z_registry_host}" \
    || buc_die "Container registry authentication failed during auto-summon"

  docker pull "${z_image_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull image: ${z_image_ref}"

  buc_info "Auto-summoned: ${z_image_ref}"
}

######################################################################
# Charge Invariant — Network Collision Detection

# Die if any compose-managed network with our nameplate exists from a foreign
# project. Catches pre-prefix-era residue (project=tadmor without RBRR
# prefix) and any other compose project whose name is the bare moniker or
# ends with "-{moniker}". Match is by labels — independent of how compose
# joined project + network into the network name.
zrbob_detect_nameplate_collision() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  local -r z_networks_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_collision_networks.txt"
  local -r z_networks_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_collision_networks_stderr.txt"
  local -r z_inspect_prefix="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_collision_inspect_"

  ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network ls \
    --filter 'label=com.docker.compose.project' \
    --filter 'label=com.docker.compose.network' \
    --format '{{.Name}}' \
    > "${z_networks_file}" 2>"${z_networks_stderr}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to list Docker networks for collision detection — see ${z_networks_stderr}"

  local z_names=()
  local z_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    test -n "${z_line}" || continue
    z_names+=("${z_line}")
  done < "${z_networks_file}"

  local z_offenders=()
  local z_index=0

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
#
# Recipe Bottle base compose file — static infrastructure
#
# Invoked via:
#   docker compose --env-file .rbk/rbrr.env --env-file .rbk/{moniker}/rbrn.env \
#     -f .rbk/rbob_compose.yml [-f .rbk/{moniker}/compose.yml] -p {moniker} up -d
#
# Env-file variables serve dual purpose:
#   1. YAML interpolation (${VAR} in this file — image refs, subnet, container names)
#   2. Container environment forwarding (bare names in environment: sections)
#
# Network topology:
#   sentry: transit (uplink to internet via bridge) + enclave (down to bottle)
#     Interface naming and default-route assignment are NOT controlled by compose:
#       - eth0/eth1 ordering is Docker-engine-defined (sentry IP-discovers via RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP)
#       - default route is assigned to the alphabetically-first attached network's gateway
#         (compose 'priority:' keys empirically do NOT influence this on linux Docker
#         engine 28.x or mac Docker Desktop 28.x — sentry replaces the default route
#         with one via uplink at RBJp1, see rbjs_sentry.sh)
#   pentacle: enclave only (static IP, routes through sentry)
#   bottle: shares pentacle network namespace (network_mode: service:pentacle)
#
# Health chain: sentry healthy → pentacle starts → pentacle healthy → bottle starts (sessile profile)

services:
  sentry:
    image: ${RBRD_GCP_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/rbi_hm/${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}/image:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}
    container_name: ${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX}${RBRN_MONIKER}-sentry
    privileged: true
    networks:
      transit: {}
      enclave:
        ipv4_address: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}
    ports:
      - "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}:${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}"
    environment:
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
      - RBRR_DNS_SERVER
      - RBRN_ENTRY_MODE
      - RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
      - RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
      - RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE
      - RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
      - RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE
      - RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
      - RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
      - RBJE_PROBE
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "test -f /tmp/rbjh_healthy"]
      interval: 2s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 15
      start_period: 5s

  pentacle:
    image: ${RBRD_GCP_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/rbi_hm/${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}/image:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}
    container_name: ${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX}${RBRN_MONIKER}-pentacle
    privileged: true
    networks:
      enclave:
        ipv4_address: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}
    environment:
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
    command: ["/opt/rbk/rbjp_pentacle.sh"]
    depends_on:
      sentry:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "test -f /tmp/rbjph_healthy"]
      interval: 2s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 15
      start_period: 5s

  bottle:
    image: ${RBRD_GCP_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/rbi_hm/${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}/image:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}
    container_name: ${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX}${RBRN_MONIKER}-bottle
    network_mode: "service:pentacle"
    security_opt:
      - label:disable
    cap_drop:
      - NET_RAW
      - NET_ADMIN
    environment:
      - RBOB_HOST_UID
      - RBOB_HOST_GID
      - RBRV_USER
    depends_on:
      pentacle:
        condition: service_healthy
    profiles:
      - sessile

networks:
  transit:
    driver: bridge
  enclave:
    driver: bridge
    ipam:
      config:
        - subnet: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}

content.text: I'll use a sed approach that mirrors each zrbrr_ line with a matching zrbrd_ line:

input.command:
files=$(grep -rln "zrbrr_kindle" /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/ --include="*.sh" 2>/dev/null | grep -v "rbrr_regime.sh\|rbrr_cli.sh")
for f in $files; do
  # For each match, duplicate the line with rbrd substituted.
  # Match leading whitespace then the exact identifier, and emit the line plus a mirror.
  sed -i '' -E \
    -e 's|^([[:space:]]*)source "(\$\{z_rbk_kit_dir\})/rbrr_regime\.sh"$|\1source "\2/rbrr_regime.sh"\
\1source "\2/rbrd_regime.sh"|' \
    -e 's|^([[:space:]]*)source "\$\{RBBC_rbrr_file\}"$|\1source "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"\
\1source "${RBBC_rbrd_file}"|' \
    -e 's|^([[:space:]]*)source "\$\{RBBC_rbrr_file\}"(.*)$|\1source "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"\2\
\1source "${RBBC_rbrd_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRD: ${RBBC_rbrd_file}"|' \
    -e 's|^([[:space:]]*)zrbrr_kindle$|\1zrbrr_kindle\
\1zrbrd_kindle|' \
    -e 's|^([[:space:]]*)zrbrr_enforce$|\1zrbrr_enforce\
\1zrbrd_enforce|' \
    "$f"
  echo "patched: $f"
done
input.description: Mirror zrbrr lines with zrbrd in dispatchers

content.description: Rename RBRR_* → RBRD_* at every consumer (rbgp_Payor, rbdc_DerivedConstants, rbgc_Constants, rbgd_DepotConstants, rbgv_AccessProbe, rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild, rbh0/*, test fixtures rbtdrf/rbtdrk/rbtdrp)

content.description: Lifecycle marshal generates blank rbrd.env alongside rbrr.env

content.description: Update the "future RBRD in rbi_df" comment block to reflect that RBRD ships as its own image at separate tag

content.description: README regime list/tree/appendix mention RBRD; handbook surfaces new regime category

file.content:
buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local -r z_vessel="rbev-sentry-deb-tether"

  local z_has_director=0
  local z_secrets_dir=""
  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
    z_secrets_dir=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_SECRETS_DIR") || z_secrets_dir=""
  fi
  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}" && \
     test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; then
    z_has_director=1
  fi

  local z_has_depot=0
  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
    local z_line=""
    while IFS= read -r z_line; do
      case "${z_line}" in RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER=?*) z_has_depot=1; break ;; esac
    done < "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  fi

  local z_conjure_summoned=0
  if command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    local z_project_id=""
    local z_region=""
    if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
      z_project_id=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrd_file}" "RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER") || z_project_id=""
      z_region=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrd_file}" "RBRD_GCP_REGION") || z_region=""
    fi
    if test -n "${z_region}" && test -n "${z_project_id}"; then
      local -r z_gar_prefix="${z_region}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${z_project_id}/"
      local -r z_df_out="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_IMAGES_PREFIX}5_repotag.txt"
      local -r z_df_err="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_STDERR_PREFIX}7_repotag.txt"
      if docker images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" \
           > "${z_df_out}" 2>"${z_df_err}"; then
        local z_line=""
        while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
          case "${z_line}" in
            "${z_gar_prefix}"*"${z_vessel}:c"[0-9]*) z_conjure_summoned=1; break ;;
          esac
        done < "${z_df_out}"
      fi
    fi
  fi

  buyy_link_yawp "${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}" "Vessel" "${z_vessel}"; local -r z_lk_vessel_name="${z_buym_yelp}"

  buh_section "${RBHO_TRACK_FIRST_BUILD}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "This track walks you through the complete ${RBYC_CONJURE} lifecycle:"
  buh_line "provision the builder toolchain, ${RBYC_ORDAIN} your first ${RBYC_VESSEL} via"
  buh_line "Cloud Build, inspect the result, pull it locally, and clean up."
  buh_e
  buh_line "You will build ${z_lk_vessel_name} — the same ${RBYC_SENTRY} you"
  buh_line "already know from the ${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} track, but this time built by"
  buh_line "Google Cloud Build with full SLSA ${RBYC_PROVENANCE}."
  buh_e

  buh_line "Prerequisites:"
  buh_e
  if test "${z_has_director}" = "1"; then
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}${RBYC_DIRECTOR} credential installed"
  else
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}${RBYC_DIRECTOR} credential missing — run:"
    buh_tt "      " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_CRED_DIRECTOR}"
  fi
  if test "${z_has_depot}" = "1"; then
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}${RBYC_DEPOT} configured (RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER populated)"
  else
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}${RBYC_DEPOT} not configured — the ${RBYC_PAYOR} must establish the ${RBYC_DEPOT}:"
    buh_tt "      " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_PAYOR_HB}"
  fi
  buh_e

file.content:
local -r z_vessel="rbev-graft-demo"
  local -r z_vessel_rbrv="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_vessel}/rbrv.env"
  local -r z_local_tag="graft-busybox:local"

  local z_has_director=0
  local z_secrets_dir=""
  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
    z_secrets_dir=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_SECRETS_DIR") || z_secrets_dir=""
  fi
  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}" && \
     test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; then
    z_has_director=1
  fi

  local z_has_depot=0
  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
    local z_line=""
    while IFS= read -r z_line; do
      case "${z_line}" in RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER=?*) z_has_depot=1; break ;; esac
    done < "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  fi

  local z_vessel_ready=0
  if test -f "${z_vessel_rbrv}"; then
    local z_mode=""
    z_mode=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_vessel_rbrv}" "RBRV_VESSEL_MODE") || z_mode=""
    test "${z_mode}" = "graft" && z_vessel_ready=1
  fi

  local z_vessel_yoked=0
  if test -f "${z_vessel_rbrv}"; then
    local z_vessel_stamp=""
    z_vessel_stamp=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_vessel_rbrv}" "RBRV_RELIQUARY") || z_vessel_stamp=""
    test -n "${z_vessel_stamp}" && z_vessel_yoked=1
  fi

  local z_grafted_installed=0
  if command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    local z_project_id=""
    local z_region=""
    if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
      z_project_id=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrd_file}" "RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER") || z_project_id=""
      z_region=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrd_file}" "RBRD_GCP_REGION") || z_region=""
    fi
    if test -n "${z_region}" && test -n "${z_project_id}"; then
      local -r z_gar_prefix="${z_region}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${z_project_id}/"
      local -r z_df_out="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_IMAGES_PREFIX}9_graft_repotag.txt"
      local -r z_df_err="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_STDERR_PREFIX}9_graft_repotag.txt"
      if docker images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" \
           > "${z_df_out}" 2>"${z_df_err}"; then

            "${z_gar_prefix}"*"${z_vessel}:g"[0-9]*) z_grafted_installed=1; break ;;

rbho_crash_course() {
  zrbho_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Configure your Repo's Environment — tabtargets, regimes, station setup, logs"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local z_rbrr_project=""
  local z_rbrr_populated=0
  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
    z_rbrr_project=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrd_file}" "RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER") || z_rbrr_project=""
    test -n "${z_rbrr_project}" && z_rbrr_populated=1
  fi

  local z_station_present=0
  local z_log_dir=""
  if test -n "${BURD_STATION_FILE:-}" && test -f "${BURD_STATION_FILE}"; then
    z_station_present=1
    z_log_dir=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${BURD_STATION_FILE}" "BURS_LOG_DIR") || z_log_dir=""
  fi

  buh_section "Recipe Bottle — Configure your Repo's Environment"
  buh_e
  buh_step_style "Step " " — "

  buh_step1 "What you ran to get here"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The command you just ran is a ${RBYC_TABTARGET} — a launcher script"
  buh_line "in the ${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR}/ directory. Tab completion narrows by prefix: type \`${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR}/rbw-<TAB>\` to see every"
  buh_line "${RBYC_RECIPE_BOTTLE} command."
  buh_e

  buh_step1 "View the project config"
  buh_e
  buh_line "A ${RBYC_REGIME} is a configuration file with a schema, a renderer,"
  buh_line "and a validator. Run the renderer for the project config regime:"
  buh_e
  buh_tt   "   " "${BUWZ_RC_RENDER}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_BURC} is checked into git — shared project settings that every"
  buh_line "clone gets. It tells the launcher where to find tools and where"
  buh_line "to look for your personal station file."
  buh_e

  buh_step1 "View your personal station"

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Handbook Onboarding - Crash Course (configure repo environment)

set -euo pipefail

test -z "${ZRBHOCC_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZRBHOCC_SOURCED=1

rbho_crash_course() {
  zrbho_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Configure your Repo's Environment — tabtargets, regimes, station setup, logs"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local z_rbrr_project=""
  local z_rbrr_populated=0
  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
    z_rbrr_project=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrd_file}" "RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER") || z_rbrr_project=""
    test -n "${z_rbrr_project}" && z_rbrr_populated=1
  fi

  local z_station_present=0
  local z_log_dir=""
  if test -n "${BURD_STATION_FILE:-}" && test -f "${BURD_STATION_FILE}"; then
    z_station_present=1
    z_log_dir=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${BURD_STATION_FILE}" "BURS_LOG_DIR") || z_log_dir=""
  fi

  buh_section "Recipe Bottle — Configure your Repo's Environment"
  buh_e
  buh_step_style "Step " " — "

  buh_step1 "What you ran to get here"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The command you just ran is a ${RBYC_TABTARGET} — a launcher script"
  buh_line "in the ${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR}/ directory. Tab completion narrows by prefix: type \`${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR}/rbw-<TAB>\` to see every"
  buh_line "${RBYC_RECIPE_BOTTLE} command."
  buh_e

  buh_step1 "View the project config"
  buh_e
  buh_line "A ${RBYC_REGIME} is a configuration file with a schema, a renderer,"
  buh_line "and a validator. Run the renderer for the project config regime:"
  buh_e
  buh_tt   "   " "${BUWZ_RC_RENDER}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_BURC} is checked into git — shared project settings that every"
  buh_line "clone gets. It tells the launcher where to find tools and where"
  buh_line "to look for your personal station file."
  buh_e

  buh_step1 "View your personal station"
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_BURS} is your per-developer station file: local, gitignored,"
  buh_line "holds things that vary per machine. Run the renderer:"
  buh_e
  buh_tt   "   " "${BUWZ_RS_RENDER}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The repo-vs-personal split is deliberate: ${RBYC_BURC} travels with the code; ${RBYC_BURS} stays on your machine."
  buh_e

  buh_step1 "Validate your station"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Every ${RBYC_REGIME} has a validate tabtarget that checks the file against"
  buh_line "its schema. This may fail if your station file is missing fields"
  buh_line "beyond the minimum the launcher required — that is expected."
  buh_line "Run it:"
  buh_e
  buh_tt   "   " "${BUWZ_RS_VALIDATE}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Read the error if it fails — it names the field and tells you"
  buh_line "what to fill in."
  buh_e
  if test "${z_station_present}" = "1"; then
    buyy_cmd_yawp " [*] ";              local -r z_mark="${z_buym_yelp}"
    buyy_cmd_yawp "${BURD_STATION_FILE}"; local -r z_path="${z_buym_yelp}"
    buh_line "${z_mark} Station file present at ${z_path}"
  else
    zrbho_po_status 0 "Station file not found"
  fi
  buh_e

  buh_step1 "Validate the repo regime"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The repository regime (${RBYC_RBRR}) holds your team's ${RBYC_DEPOT}"
  buh_line "identity — the GCP project where container images are built and stored."
  buh_line "Run the validator:"
  buh_e
  buh_tt   "   " "${RBZ_VALIDATE_REPO}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "On a bare fork, ${RBYC_RBRR} fields are blank and validation will fail —"
  buh_line "you need a ${RBYC_PAYOR} account and a ${RBYC_DEPOT} to populate them."
  buh_line "On a team repo, they are already populated and validation passes."
  buh_line "Either way, read the output — it tells you exactly what state you're in."
  buh_e
  if test "${z_rbrr_populated}" = "1"; then
    zrbho_po_status 1 "RBRR populated — depot project: ${z_rbrr_project}"
  else
    zrbho_po_status 0 "RBRR not populated — depot identity fields are blank"
  fi
  buh_e

  buh_step1 "Check your logs"
  buh_e
  buh_line "When you ran the validator, it printed file paths at the top"
  buh_line "of its output. Every state-changing command writes three ${RBYC_LOG}"
  buh_line "files to ${RBYC_BURS}_LOG_DIR:"
  buh_e
  if test -n "${z_log_dir}"; then
    buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_log_dir}/${BURC_LOG_LAST}.${BURC_LOG_EXT}"; local -r z_log_path="${z_buym_yelp}"
    buh_line "   stable    ${z_log_path}  (always the same path, great for Claude)"
  else
    buh_line "   stable    always the same path — tooling reads this one"
  fi
  buh_line "   per-cmd   same filename across runs — diff between executions"
  buh_line "   history   timestamped — permanent record, never overwritten"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Some commands also write a ${RBYC_TRANSCRIPT} — a single file"
  buh_line "capturing key decision points and state transitions. When a"
  buh_line "command fails, the transcript is the first thing to read."
  buh_e
  buh_line "Handbook display commands (like this one) do not log — teaching"
  buh_line "output is ephemeral by design."
  buh_e

  buh_step1 "The pattern"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Every ${RBYC_REGIME} has a render and a validate tabtarget."
  buh_line "The letter after \`r\` is all that changes:"
  buh_e
  buyy_tt_yawp "${BUWZ_RC_RENDER}";       local -r z_rc_r="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${BUWZ_RC_VALIDATE}";      local -r z_rc_v="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${BUWZ_RS_RENDER}";        local -r z_rs_r="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${BUWZ_RS_VALIDATE}";      local -r z_rs_v="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_RENDER_REPO}";       local -r z_rr_r="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_VALIDATE_REPO}";     local -r z_rr_v="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_RENDER_PAYOR}";      local -r z_rp_r="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_VALIDATE_PAYOR}";    local -r z_rp_v="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_RENDER_OAUTH}";      local -r z_ro_r="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_VALIDATE_OAUTH}";    local -r z_ro_v="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_RENDER_VESSEL}";     local -r z_rv_r="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_VALIDATE_VESSEL}";   local -r z_rv_v="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_RENDER_NAMEPLATE}";  local -r z_rn_r="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_VALIDATE_NAMEPLATE}";local -r z_rn_v="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_RENDER_AUTH}";       local -r z_ra_r="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_VALIDATE_AUTH}";     local -r z_ra_v="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line "   c  ${RBYC_BURC}  ${z_rc_r}   ${z_rc_v}"
  buh_line "   s  ${RBYC_BURS}  ${z_rs_r}  ${z_rs_v}"
  buh_line "   r  ${RBYC_RBRR}  ${z_rr_r}     ${z_rr_v}"
  buh_line "   p  ${RBYC_RBRP}  ${z_rp_r}    ${z_rp_v}"
  buh_line "   o  ${RBYC_RBRO}  ${z_ro_r}    ${z_ro_v}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "These take a target name (vessel, nameplate, or role):"
  buh_e
  buh_line "   v  ${RBYC_RBRV}  ${z_rv_r}     ${z_rv_v}"
  buh_line "   n  ${RBYC_RBRN}  ${z_rn_r}  ${z_rn_v}"
  buh_line "   a  ${RBYC_RBRA}  ${z_ra_r}       ${z_ra_v}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Learn the letter — you can find any regime's tools from it."
  buh_e

  buh_step1 "Next steps"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Your repo environment is configured. The tools work, errors explain"
  buh_line "themselves, and ${RBYC_LOGS} land where you told them to."
  buh_e
  buh_line "Return to the start menu for what to do next:"
  buh_tt   "   " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
  buh_e
}

lines: buc_doc_brief "Configure your Repo's Environment — tabtargets, regimes, station setup, logs"

content.subject: Investigate archetype + survey consumers
content.description: Read sibling regime archetype files (rblm_cli, rbgc, RBSRR, RBSRP, RBSDE) and survey all consumers of the four moved vars

content.description: Create the regime/cli pair following the sibling archetype with the four moved variables

content.subject: Update rbrr_regime.sh to remove four moved vars
content.description: Strip CLOUD_PREFIX, DEPOT_MONIKER, GCP_REGION, GCB_MACHINE_TYPE plus their custom format checks (and joint-length cap)

content.subject: Migrate consumers to RBRD_*
content.description: Rename RBRR_* → RBRD_* at every consumer (rbgp_Payor, rbdc_DerivedConstants, rbgc_Constants, rbgd_DepotConstants, rbgv_AccessProbe, rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild, rbh0/*, test fixtures rbtdrf/rbtdrk/rbtdrp)

content.subject: Create RBSRT-RegimeDepot.adoc spec
content.description: New spec paralleling RBSRP/RBSRR; update RBS0 index

content.subject: Update affected spec/adoc files
content.description: RBSDE-depot_levy, RBSRR-RegimeRepo (strip), RBSDY, RBSDI, RBSDK, RBSRK, RBSAJ, RBSIA, RBSIR, RBSIW, RBSTB, RBSGM — anywhere RBRR_{four vars} appear

content.subject: Update rblm marshal to emit rbrd.env blank
content.description: Lifecycle marshal generates blank rbrd.env alongside rbrr.env

content.subject: Add rbw-rdr / rbw-rdv tabtargets via zipper
content.description: Enroll Render and Validate depot regime commands

content.subject: Refresh stale rbi_df comment in rbgc_Constants
content.description: Update the "future RBRD in rbi_df" comment block to reflect that RBRD ships as its own image at separate tag

content.subject: Update README + handbook
content.description: README regime list/tree/appendix mention RBRD; handbook surfaces new regime category

content.subject: Validate by running rbw-rdr/rbw-rdv + qualify-fast
content.description: Run validation tabtargets to confirm clean operation, then qualify-fast or test suite

input.description: Check for remaining references to renamed locals

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.

# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#

#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Handbook Onboarding - Crash Course (configure repo environment)

set -euo pipefail

test -z "${ZRBHOCC_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZRBHOCC_SOURCED=1

rbho_crash_course() {
  zrbho_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Configure your Repo's Environment — tabtargets, regimes, station setup, logs"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local z_rbrd_project=""
  local z_rbrd_populated=0
  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrd_file}"; then
    z_rbrd_project=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrd_file}" "RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER") || z_rbrd_project=""
    test -n "${z_rbrd_project}" && z_rbrd_populated=1
  fi

  local z_station_present=0
  local z_log_dir=""
  if test -n "${BURD_STATION_FILE:-}" && test -f "${BURD_STATION_FILE}"; then
    z_station_present=1
    z_log_dir=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${BURD_STATION_FILE}" "BURS_LOG_DIR") || z_log_dir=""
  fi

  buh_section "Recipe Bottle — Configure your Repo's Environment"
  buh_e
  buh_step_style "Step " " — "

  buh_step1 "What you ran to get here"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The command you just ran is a ${RBYC_TABTARGET} — a launcher script"
  buh_line "in the ${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR}/ directory. Tab completion narrows by prefix: type \`${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR}/rbw-<TAB>\` to see every"
  buh_line "${RBYC_RECIPE_BOTTLE} command."
  buh_e

  buh_step1 "View the project config"
  buh_e
  buh_line "A ${RBYC_REGIME} is a configuration file with a schema, a renderer,"
  buh_line "and a validator. Run the renderer for the project config regime:"
  buh_e
  buh_tt   "   " "${BUWZ_RC_RENDER}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_BURC} is checked into git — shared project settings that every"
  buh_line "clone gets. It tells the launcher where to find tools and where"
  buh_line "to look for your personal station file."
  buh_e

  buh_step1 "View your personal station"
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_BURS} is your per-developer station file: local, gitignored,"
  buh_line "holds things that vary per machine. Run the renderer:"
  buh_e
  buh_tt   "   " "${BUWZ_RS_RENDER}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The repo-vs-personal split is deliberate: ${RBYC_BURC} travels with the code; ${RBYC_BURS} stays on your machine."
  buh_e

  buh_step1 "Validate your station"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Every ${RBYC_REGIME} has a validate tabtarget that checks the file against"
  buh_line "its schema. This may fail if your station file is missing fields"
  buh_line "beyond the minimum the launcher required — that is expected."
  buh_line "Run it:"
  buh_e
  buh_tt   "   " "${BUWZ_RS_VALIDATE}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Read the error if it fails — it names the field and tells you"
  buh_line "what to fill in."
  buh_e
  if test "${z_station_present}" = "1"; then
    buyy_cmd_yawp " [*] ";              local -r z_mark="${z_buym_yelp}"
    buyy_cmd_yawp "${BURD_STATION_FILE}"; local -r z_path="${z_buym_yelp}"
    buh_line "${z_mark} Station file present at ${z_path}"
  else
    zrbho_po_status 0 "Station file not found"
  fi
  buh_e

  buh_step1 "Validate the repo regime"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The repository regime (${RBYC_RBRR}) holds your team's ${RBYC_DEPOT}"
  buh_line "identity — the GCP project where container images are built and stored."
  buh_line "Run the validator:"
  buh_e
  buh_tt   "   " "${RBZ_VALIDATE_REPO}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "On a bare fork, ${RBYC_RBRR} fields are blank and validation will fail —"
  buh_line "you need a ${RBYC_PAYOR} account and a ${RBYC_DEPOT} to populate them."
  buh_line "On a team repo, they are already populated and validation passes."
  buh_line "Either way, read the output — it tells you exactly what state you're in."
  buh_e
  if test "${z_rbrr_populated}" = "1"; then
    zrbho_po_status 1 "RBRR populated — depot project: ${z_rbrr_project}"
  else
    zrbho_po_status 0 "RBRR not populated — depot identity fields are blank"
  fi
  buh_e

  buh_step1 "Check your logs"
  buh_e
  buh_line "When you ran the validator, it printed file paths at the top"
  buh_line "of its output. Every state-changing command writes three ${RBYC_LOG}"
  buh_line "files to ${RBYC_BURS}_LOG_DIR:"
  buh_e
  if test -n "${z_log_dir}"; then
    buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_log_dir}/${BURC_LOG_LAST}.${BURC_LOG_EXT}"; local -r z_log_path="${z_buym_yelp}"
    buh_line "   stable    ${z_log_path}  (always the same path, great for Claude)"
  else
    buh_line "   stable    always the same path — tooling reads this one"
  fi
  buh_line "   per-cmd   same filename across runs — diff between executions"
  buh_line "   history   timestamped — permanent record, never overwritten"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Some commands also write a ${RBYC_TRANSCRIPT} — a single file"
  buh_line "capturing key decision points and state transitions. When a"
  buh_line "command fails, the transcript is the first thing to read."
  buh_e
  buh_line "Handbook display commands (like this one) do not log — teaching"
  buh_line "output is ephemeral by design."
  buh_e

  buh_step1 "The pattern"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Every ${RBYC_REGIME} has a render and a validate tabtarget."
  buh_line "The letter after \`r\` is all that changes:"
  buh_e
  buyy_tt_yawp "${BUWZ_RC_RENDER}";       local -r z_rc_r="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${BUWZ_RC_VALIDATE}";      local -r z_rc_v="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${BUWZ_RS_RENDER}";        local -r z_rs_r="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${BUWZ_RS_VALIDATE}";      local -r z_rs_v="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_RENDER_REPO}";       local -r z_rr_r="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_VALIDATE_REPO}";     local -r z_rr_v="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_RENDER_PAYOR}";      local -r z_rp_r="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_VALIDATE_PAYOR}";    local -r z_rp_v="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_RENDER_OAUTH}";      local -r z_ro_r="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_VALIDATE_OAUTH}";    local -r z_ro_v="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_RENDER_VESSEL}";     local -r z_rv_r="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_VALIDATE_VESSEL}";   local -r z_rv_v="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_RENDER_NAMEPLATE}";  local -r z_rn_r="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_VALIDATE_NAMEPLATE}";local -r z_rn_v="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_RENDER_AUTH}";       local -r z_ra_r="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_VALIDATE_AUTH}";     local -r z_ra_v="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line "   c  ${RBYC_BURC}  ${z_rc_r}   ${z_rc_v}"
  buh_line "   s  ${RBYC_BURS}  ${z_rs_r}  ${z_rs_v}"
  buh_line "   r  ${RBYC_RBRR}  ${z_rr_r}     ${z_rr_v}"
  buh_line "   p  ${RBYC_RBRP}  ${z_rp_r}    ${z_rp_v}"
  buh_line "   o  ${RBYC_RBRO}  ${z_ro_r}    ${z_ro_v}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "These take a target name (vessel, nameplate, or role):"
  buh_e
  buh_line "   v  ${RBYC_RBRV}  ${z_rv_r}     ${z_rv_v}"
  buh_line "   n  ${RBYC_RBRN}  ${z_rn_r}  ${z_rn_v}"
  buh_line "   a  ${RBYC_RBRA}  ${z_ra_r}       ${z_ra_v}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Learn the letter — you can find any regime's tools from it."
  buh_e

  buh_step1 "Next steps"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Your repo environment is configured. The tools work, errors explain"
  buh_line "themselves, and ${RBYC_LOGS} land where you told them to."
  buh_e
  buh_line "Return to the start menu for what to do next:"
  buh_tt   "   " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
  buh_e
}

# eof

file.content:
# Discover secrets dir and vessel dir for pre-confirmation inventory
  local z_secrets_dir=""
  local z_vessel_dir=""
  local z_secrets_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_secrets_line || test -n "${z_secrets_line}"; do
    case "${z_secrets_line}" in
      RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=*) z_secrets_dir="${z_secrets_line#RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=}" ;;
      RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=*)  z_vessel_dir="${z_secrets_line#RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=}"  ;;
    esac
  done < "${z_rbrr}"

  buh_section "Marshal Zero"
  buh_line "  Targets: ${z_rbrr}"
  buh_line "           ${z_rbrd}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  RBRR fields blanked (zeroed to onboarding start):"
  buh_line "    RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  RBRR fields pre-filled to defaults:"
  buh_line "    RBRR_DNS_SERVER, RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT,"
  buh_line "    RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS,"
  buh_line "    RBRR_VESSEL_DIR, RBRR_SECRETS_DIR"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  RBRD fields blanked (depot identity — operator decision):"
  buh_line "    RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  RBRD fields pre-filled to defaults:"
  buh_line "    RBRD_GCP_REGION, RBRD_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  Depot credentials DELETED (tied to prior depot):"
  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}"; then
    local z_preview=""
    local z_any_cred=0
    for z_preview in "${RBCC_role_governor}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_retriever}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_assay}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; do
      if test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${z_preview}"; then
        buh_line "    ${z_secrets_dir}/${z_preview}"
        z_any_cred=1
      fi
    done
    test "${z_any_cred}" = "1" || buh_line "    (none present)"
  else
    buh_line "    (secrets dir not configured)"
  fi
  buh_e
  buh_line "  Vessel hallmarks BLANKED (stale after depot change):"
  local z_np_preview=""
  local z_any_np=0
  for z_np_preview in "${RBBC_dot_dir}"/*/rbrn.env; do
    test -f "${z_np_preview}" || continue
    buh_line "    ${z_np_preview}"
    z_any_np=1
  done
  test "${z_any_np}" = "1" || buh_line "    (no nameplates found)"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  Vessel regime fields BLANKED (depot-scoped, stale after depot change):"
  buh_line "    RBRV_RELIQUARY, RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR in all rbrv.env"
  if test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" && test -d "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
    local z_vr_preview=""
    local z_any_vr=0
    for z_vr_preview in "${z_vessel_dir}"/*/rbrv.env; do
      test -f "${z_vr_preview}" || continue
      buh_line "    ${z_vr_preview}"
      z_any_vr=1
    done
    test "${z_any_vr}" = "1" || buh_line "    (no vessel regimes found)"
  else
    buh_line "    (vessel dir not configured or missing)"
  fi
  buh_e
  buh_line "  Preserved (payor-scoped, survives depot change):"
  buh_line "    ${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_payor}/${RBCC_rbro_file}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  On completion, marshal-zero auto-commits the in-tree mutations."
  buh_e
  buc_require "Proceed with marshal zero?" "zero"

file.content:
local z_vr_tmp=""
    for z_vr in "${z_vessel_dir}"/*/rbrv.env; do
      test -f "${z_vr}" || continue
      z_vr_tmp="${z_vr}.tmp"
      while IFS= read -r z_line; do
        case "${z_line}" in
          RBRV_RELIQUARY=*)       printf '%s\n' "RBRV_RELIQUARY="       ;;
          RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR=*)  printf '%s\n' "${z_line%%=*}="        ;;
          *)                      printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"             ;;
        esac
      done < "${z_vr}" > "${z_vr_tmp}" && mv "${z_vr_tmp}" "${z_vr}"
      buh_line "  Blanked depot-scoped fields: ${z_vr}"
    done
  fi

  buh_line "  Zero complete: ${z_rbrr}"
  buh_e

  # Auto-commit the in-tree mutations so post-zero state is captured as a single
  # commit. RBRA deletions (outside repo) are not staged.
  buc_step "Committing marshal-zero state"
  git add "${z_rbrr}" || buc_die "Failed to stage RBRR file"

  local z_stage=""
  for z_stage in "${RBBC_dot_dir}"/*/rbrn.env; do
    test -f "${z_stage}" || continue
    git add "${z_stage}" || buc_die "Failed to stage: ${z_stage}"
  done
  if test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" && test -d "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
    for z_stage in "${z_vessel_dir}"/*/rbrv.env; do
      test -f "${z_stage}" || continue
      git add "${z_stage}" || buc_die "Failed to stage: ${z_stage}"
    done
  fi

  if git diff --cached --quiet; then
    buh_line "  No changes to commit — already in marshal-zero state"
  else
    git commit -m "Marshal Zero — release qualification reset" || buc_die "Marshal-zero commit failed"
    buh_line "  Marshal-zero state committed"
  fi
  buh_e

  buh_line "  Next: verify onboarding guide detects blank state:"
  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
  buc_success "Regime zeroed to blank template"
}

2710	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
2711	{rbrs_prefix}::
2712	Recipe Bottle Station Regime
2713	
2714	=== Repository Regime (RBRR)
2715	
2716	The
2717	regime
2718	defines installation-wide configuration that rarely changes and is shared across all
2719	{at_recipe_bottle}
2720	operations.
2721	This specification documents only the subset of RBRR variables relevant to
2722	{gcp_service}
2723	operations.
2724	
2725	[[rbrr_regime]]
2726	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton
2727	{rbrr_regime}::
2728	Repository-wide configuration and installation-wide settings shared across all
2729	{at_recipe_bottle}
2730	operations.
2731	
2732	[[rbrd_gcp_region]]
2733	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtg_region
2734	{rbrd_gcp_region}::
2735	Default
2736	{gcp_service}
2737	region for regional services like
2738	{gar_service}
2739	and
2740	{gcb_service}.
2741	
2742	[[rbrr_secrets_dir]]
2743	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
2744	{rbrr_secrets_dir}::
2745	Directory containing service account credential files for all operational roles.
2746	Validated to exist before credential file paths are derived.
2747	
2748	[[rbrr_retriever_rbra_file]]
2749	//axl_voices axrd_constructed axtu_path
2750	{rbrr_retriever_rbra_file}::
2751	Path to the
2752	{rbtr_retriever}
2753	{rbtgi_rbra_file}
2754	for container image operations.
2755	Derived from
2756	{rbrr_secrets_dir}
2757	as `retriever/rbra.env`.
2758	
2759	[[rbrr_director_rbra_file]]
2760	//axl_voices axrd_constructed axtu_path
2761	{rbrr_director_rbra_file}::
2762	Path to the
2763	{rbtr_director}
2764	{rbtgi_rbra_file}
2765	for build submission and image management.
2766	Derived from
2767	{rbrr_secrets_dir}
2768	as `director/rbra.env`.
2769	
2770	[[rbrr_governor_rbra_file]]
2771	//axl_voices axrd_constructed axtu_path
2772	{rbrr_governor_rbra_file}::
2773	Path to the
2774	{rbtr_governor}
2775	{rbtgi_rbra_file}
2776	for
2777	{gcp_project_id}
2778	administration.
2779	Derived from
2780	{rbrr_secrets_dir}
2781	as `governor/rbra.env`.
2782	
2783	[[rbrr_payor_rbro_file]]
2784	//axl_voices axrd_constructed axtu_path
2785	{rbrr_payor_rbro_file}::
2786	Path to the
2787	{rbtr_payor}
2788	{rbro_regime}
2789	credentials file for OAuth operations.
2790	Derived from
2791	{rbrr_secrets_dir}
2792	as `payor/rbro.env`.
2793	
2794	[[rbrr_bottle_workspace]]
2795	//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_path
2796	{rbrr_bottle_workspace}::
2797	Container-internal working directory path for all
2798	{at_bottle_container_s}.
2799	Conventionally `/workspace`, following the industry-standard devcontainer convention.
2800	Used by compose `working_dir`, volume mount targets, and bottle engagement tabtargets.
2801	
2802	[[rbrr_vessel_dir]]
2803	//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_path
2804	{rbrr_vessel_dir}::
2805	Directory containing vessel configuration subdirectories for
2806	{rbrr_regime}.
2807	
2808	[[rbrr_dns_server]]
2809	//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_ipv4
2810	{rbrr_dns_server}::
2811	DNS server IP address for sentry container name resolution.
2812	
2813	[[rbrm_ignite_machine_name]]
2814	//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_xname
2815	{rbrm_ignite_machine_name}::
2816	Podman machine name for bottle ignition operations.
2817	
2818	[[rbrm_deploy_machine_name]]
2819	//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_xname
2820	{rbrm_deploy_machine_name}::
2821	Podman machine name for bottle deployment operations.
2822	
2823	[[rbrm_crane_tar_gz]]
2824	//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_path
2825	{rbrm_crane_tar_gz}::
2826	Path to crane tool tarball for OCI registry operations.
2827	
2828	[[rbrm_manifest_platforms]]
2829	//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_platform_list
2830	{rbrm_manifest_platforms}::
2831	Space-separated target platforms for multi-platform manifest creation.
2832	
2833	[[rbrm_chosen_podman_version]]
2834	//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_version
2835	{rbrm_chosen_podman_version}::
2836	Podman version string for VM provisioning.
2837	
2838	[[rbrm_chosen_vmimage_origin]]
2839	//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_string
2840	{rbrm_chosen_vmimage_origin}::
2841	Podman VM image origin specification.
2842	
2843	[[rbrm_chosen_identity]]
2844	//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_string
2845	{rbrm_chosen_identity}::
2846	Identity specification for VM or build environment.
2847	
2848	[[rbrd_gcb_machine_type]]
2849	//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_string
2850	{rbrd_gcb_machine_type}::
2851	Compute Engine machine type for the private worker pool, consumed at pool creation time by
2852	{rbtgo_depot_levy}.
2853	Default: `e2-standard-2`.
2854	The pool resource holds this value; individual build requests do not specify machine type.
2855	
2856	NOTE: *Private pool is always required.*
2857	Private pools are a prerequisite for VPC Service Controls and `NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS` hardening (see `RBSCB-CloudBuildPosture.adoc`).
2858	Pricing is region-dependent: in us-central1/us-east4, private pool e2-standard-2 matches the default pool rate ($0.006/build-minute); in other regions the private pool rate is up to 1.4× higher.
2859	This cost is accepted because the security prerequisite is non-negotiable and maintaining two code paths (conditional pool vs. default) doubles configuration surface.
2860	+
2861	Cloud Build supports two distinct machine type naming systems:
2862	the default shared pool uses Cloud Build enum values (`E2_HIGHCPU_8`, `N1_HIGHCPU_32`) in `options.machineType` of the build JSON;
2863	private pools use Compute Engine instance type names (`e2-standard-2`, `n2d-standard-4`, `c3-standard-8`) in `workerConfig.machineType` at pool creation.
2864	Valid private pool families: `e2`, `n2d`, `c3` (availability varies by region).
2865	Since Recipe Bottle uses only private pools, only the Compute Engine format is valid for
2866	{rbrd_gcb_machine_type}.
2867	
2868	[[rbrr_gcb_min_concurrent_builds]]
2869	//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_integer
2870	{rbrr_gcb_min_concurrent_builds}::
2871	Minimum concurrent Cloud Build execution count.
2872	
2873	[[rbrr_gcb_timeout]]
2874	//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_duration
2875	{rbrr_gcb_timeout}::
2876	Maximum duration for Cloud Build operations.
2877	
2878	[[rbrr_public_docs_url]]
2879	//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_url
2880	{rbrr_public_docs_url}::
2881	Public docs URL — readme target for buh_tlt glossary links, updated per-release or per-incorporation.
2882	
2883	// RBRG variable definitions eliminated — reliquaries replace pin-based tool image management
2884	// Tool images (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft, binfmt, skopeo) now sourced from reliquary at ordain time
2885	
2886	[[rbrr_group_public_docs]]
2887	//axvr_group
2888	{rbrr_group_public_docs}::
2889	Public documentation URL for user-facing references.
2890	
2891	[[rbrr_group_service_accounts]]
2892	//axvr_group
2893	{rbrr_group_service_accounts}::
2894	Credential file paths derived from
2895	{rbrr_secrets_dir}
2896	for each operational role.
2897	
2898	[[rbrr_group_container_runtime]]
2899	//axvr_group
2900	{rbrr_group_container_runtime}::
2901	Container runtime and deployment environment settings.
2902	
2903	[[rbrr_group_build_configuration]]
2904	//axvr_group
2905	{rbrr_group_build_configuration}::
2906	Container image build process configuration.
2907	
2908	[[rbrr_group_cbv2]]
2909	//axvr_group
2910	{rbrr_group_cbv2}::
2911	include::RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc[]
2912	
2913	// RBRG regime eliminated — reliquaries replace pin-based tool image management
2914	// Tool images now sourced from datestamped reliquary namespace in GAR at ordain time
2915	
2916	=== Recipe Bottle GCP Constants (RBGC)
2917	
2918	Pure-literal tinder constants used in resource-name composition.
2919	Source-time available — no kindle ordering dependency.
2920	
2921	[[rbgc_depot_project_infix]]
2922	{rbgc_depot_project_infix}::
2923	Pure-literal infix `"d-"` placed between
2924	{rbrd_cloud_prefix}
2925	and
2926	{rbrd_depot_moniker}
2927	to materialise the depot project ID at `RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` derivation time.
2928	Composition rule: `{rbrd_cloud_prefix}{rbgc_depot_project_infix}{rbrd_depot_moniker}`.
2929	Consumed by
2930	{rbtgo_depot_unmake}'s
2931	live-disqualify refusal step and by
2932	{rbtgo_depot_list}'s
2933	displayName discriminator.
2934	
2935	=== Machine Regime (RBRM)
2936	
2937	[[rbrm_regime]]
2938	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton
2939	{rbrm_regime}::

file.content:
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{rbrs_prefix}::
Recipe Bottle Station Regime

=== Repository Regime (RBRR)

The
regime
defines installation-wide configuration that rarely changes and is shared across all
{at_recipe_bottle}
operations.
This specification documents only the subset of RBRR variables relevant to
{gcp_service}
operations.

[[rbrr_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton
{rbrr_regime}::
Repository-wide configuration and installation-wide settings shared across all
{at_recipe_bottle}
operations.

[[rbrd_gcp_region]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtg_region
{rbrd_gcp_region}::
Default
{gcp_service}
region for regional services like
{gar_service}
and
{gcb_service}.

[[rbrr_secrets_dir]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{rbrr_secrets_dir}::
Directory containing service account credential files for all operational roles.
Validated to exist before credential file paths are derived.

[[rbrr_retriever_rbra_file]]
//axl_voices axrd_constructed axtu_path
{rbrr_retriever_rbra_file}::
Path to the
{rbtr_retriever}
{rbtgi_rbra_file}
for container image operations.
Derived from
{rbrr_secrets_dir}
as `retriever/rbra.env`.

[[rbrr_director_rbra_file]]
//axl_voices axrd_constructed axtu_path
{rbrr_director_rbra_file}::
Path to the
{rbtr_director}
{rbtgi_rbra_file}
for build submission and image management.
Derived from
{rbrr_secrets_dir}
as `director/rbra.env`.

[[rbrr_governor_rbra_file]]
//axl_voices axrd_constructed axtu_path
{rbrr_governor_rbra_file}::
Path to the
{rbtr_governor}
{rbtgi_rbra_file}
for
{gcp_project_id}
administration.
Derived from
{rbrr_secrets_dir}
as `governor/rbra.env`.

[[rbrr_payor_rbro_file]]
//axl_voices axrd_constructed axtu_path
{rbrr_payor_rbro_file}::
Path to the
{rbtr_payor}
{rbro_regime}
credentials file for OAuth operations.
Derived from
{rbrr_secrets_dir}
as `payor/rbro.env`.

[[rbrr_bottle_workspace]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_path
{rbrr_bottle_workspace}::
Container-internal working directory path for all
{at_bottle_container_s}.
Conventionally `/workspace`, following the industry-standard devcontainer convention.
Used by compose `working_dir`, volume mount targets, and bottle engagement tabtargets.

[[rbrr_vessel_dir]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_path
{rbrr_vessel_dir}::
Directory containing vessel configuration subdirectories for
{rbrr_regime}.

[[rbrr_dns_server]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_ipv4
{rbrr_dns_server}::
DNS server IP address for sentry container name resolution.

[[rbrm_ignite_machine_name]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_xname
{rbrm_ignite_machine_name}::
Podman machine name for bottle ignition operations.

[[rbrm_deploy_machine_name]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_xname
{rbrm_deploy_machine_name}::
Podman machine name for bottle deployment operations.

[[rbrm_crane_tar_gz]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_path
{rbrm_crane_tar_gz}::
Path to crane tool tarball for OCI registry operations.

[[rbrm_manifest_platforms]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_platform_list
{rbrm_manifest_platforms}::
Space-separated target platforms for multi-platform manifest creation.

[[rbrm_chosen_podman_version]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_version
{rbrm_chosen_podman_version}::
Podman version string for VM provisioning.

[[rbrm_chosen_vmimage_origin]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_string
{rbrm_chosen_vmimage_origin}::
Podman VM image origin specification.

[[rbrm_chosen_identity]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_string
{rbrm_chosen_identity}::
Identity specification for VM or build environment.

[[rbrd_gcb_machine_type]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_string
{rbrd_gcb_machine_type}::
Compute Engine machine type for the private worker pool, consumed at pool creation time by
{rbtgo_depot_levy}.
Default: `e2-standard-2`.
The pool resource holds this value; individual build requests do not specify machine type.

NOTE: *Private pool is always required.*
Private pools are a prerequisite for VPC Service Controls and `NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS` hardening (see `RBSCB-CloudBuildPosture.adoc`).
Pricing is region-dependent: in us-central1/us-east4, private pool e2-standard-2 matches the default pool rate ($0.006/build-minute); in other regions the private pool rate is up to 1.4× higher.
This cost is accepted because the security prerequisite is non-negotiable and maintaining two code paths (conditional pool vs. default) doubles configuration surface.
+
Cloud Build supports two distinct machine type naming systems:
the default shared pool uses Cloud Build enum values (`E2_HIGHCPU_8`, `N1_HIGHCPU_32`) in `options.machineType` of the build JSON;
private pools use Compute Engine instance type names (`e2-standard-2`, `n2d-standard-4`, `c3-standard-8`) in `workerConfig.machineType` at pool creation.
Valid private pool families: `e2`, `n2d`, `c3` (availability varies by region).
Since Recipe Bottle uses only private pools, only the Compute Engine format is valid for
{rbrd_gcb_machine_type}.

1	//axhrb_regime
2	{rbrr_regime}
3	
4	This regime specifies variables that define repository-wide configuration and installation-wide settings within the Recipe Bottle system.
5	Each
6	{rbrr_regime}
7	instance represents foundational infrastructure configuration that rarely changes and is shared across all Recipe Bottle operations.
8	RBRR assignment files are found in the operator's local filesystem (typically `../station-files/rbrr.env`) and configure the depot identity tuple `(RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER)` (from which depot project ID, GAR repository, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC constants), regional settings, and service account credential file paths.
9	
10	//axhrgb_group
11	{rbrr_group_resource_prefixing}
12	
13	Operator-chosen identifiers that scope resource names across the cloud and local-runtime namespaces.
14	Enables multi-regime isolation on shared infrastructure — distinct depots or developers can coexist in the same GCP project or on the same developer workstation without name collisions.
15	All three variables must be populated — the marshal-zero path blanks them so a fresh regime requires an explicit operator decision before `rbrr_validate` passes.
16	The two prefixes must be 2–11 characters, start with a lowercase letter, and end with a hyphen (regex `^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*-$`).
17	
18	//axhrgv_variable
19	{rbrd_cloud_prefix}
20	
21	Prefix prepended to all cloud-visible resource names under the depot: GAR image paths, vessel package names,
22	{gcb_service}
23	substitutions, and other GCP-scoped identifiers.
24	
25	//axhrgv_variable
26	{rbrd_depot_moniker}
27	
28	Stem identifier that combines with
29	{rbrd_cloud_prefix}
30	to form the depot identity tuple.
31	Must be 1–26 characters, start with a lowercase letter, and contain only lowercase alphanumerics (regex `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`).
32	The tuple derives `RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM`, and `RBDC_GCS_BUCKET` at kindle time.
33	
34	//axhrgv_variable
35	{rbrr_runtime_prefix}
36	
37	Prefix prepended to machine-local resource names: container names, Docker network names, and other runtime identifiers on the operator's workstation.
38	
39	//axhrv_variable
40	{rbrd_gcp_region}
41	
42	The default
43	{gcp_service}
44	region for regional services like
45	{gar_service}
46	and
47	{gcb_service}.
48	Applied when creating repositories and submitting builds unless overridden by operation-specific configuration.
49	
50	//axhrgb_group
51	{rbrr_group_service_accounts}
52	
53	Credential file paths derived from a single enrolled directory for each operational role.
54	Each RBRA file contains the minimum identity and signing material needed for authentication to
55	{gcp_service}
56	via service account.
57	The payor RBRO file contains OAuth refresh credentials for
58	{rbtr_payor}
59	operations.
60	
61	//axhrgv_variable
62	{rbrr_secrets_dir}
63	
64	Directory on the operator's local filesystem outside the source repository that contains all credential files.
65	Enrolled and validated to exist; individual file paths are derived from this directory at lock time.
66	
67	//axhrgv_variable
68	{rbrr_retriever_rbra_file}
69	
70	Path derived from
71	{rbrr_secrets_dir}
72	as `retriever/rbra.env`.
73	Enables the operator to assume the retriever role for container image operations.
74	
75	//axhrgv_variable
76	{rbrr_director_rbra_file}
77	
78	Path derived from
79	{rbrr_secrets_dir}
80	as `director/rbra.env`.
81	Enables the operator to assume the director role for build submission and image management operations.
82	
83	//axhrgv_variable
84	{rbrr_governor_rbra_file}
85	
86	Path derived from
87	{rbrr_secrets_dir}
88	as `governor/rbra.env`.
89	Enables the operator to assume the governor role for
90	{gcp_project_id}
91	administration operations.
92	
93	//axhrgv_variable
94	{rbrr_payor_rbro_file}
95	
96	Path derived from
97	{rbrr_secrets_dir}
98	as `payor/rbro.env`.
99	Enables the operator to perform
100	{rbtr_payor}
101	OAuth operations using locally stored refresh credentials.
102	
103	//axhrgb_group
104	{rbrr_group_container_runtime}
105	
106	Variables that configure container runtime and deployment environment settings.
107	These settings control DNS resolution and vessel directory layout.
108	
109	//axhrgv_variable
110	{rbrr_vessel_dir}
111	
112	Directory path containing vessel configuration subdirectories.
113	Each subdirectory defines a vessel image source (bind or conjure mode).
114	
115	//axhrgv_variable
116	{rbrr_dns_server}
117	
118	DNS server IP address used by sentry containers for name resolution.
119	Injected into container environment to control DNS behavior.
120	
121	//axhrgb_group
122	{rbrr_group_build_configuration}
123	
124	Operator-chosen
125	{gcb_service}
126	build parameters.
127	Builder images are sourced from the reliquary;
128	platform targets and VM supply chain are in the
129	{rbrm_regime}.
130	
131	//axhrgv_variable
132	{rbrd_gcb_machine_type}
133	
134	Compute Engine machine type for the private worker pool, consumed at pool creation time by
135	{rbtgo_depot_levy}.
136	Valid families: `e2`, `n2d`, `c3`.
137	Examples: `e2-standard-2`, `e2-highcpu-8`, `n2d-standard-4`.
138	
139	//axhrgv_variable
140	{rbrr_gcb_min_concurrent_builds}
141	
142	Minimum number of concurrent Cloud Build executions required.
143	With private pools, the relevant quota metric is `concurrent_private_pool_build_cpus` on the pool's host project.
144	Machine type is determined by the pool configuration at creation time, not by individual build requests.
145	
146	//axhrgv_variable
147	{rbrr_gcb_timeout}
148	
149	Maximum duration for Cloud Build operations before timeout.
150	Specified in seconds or duration format (e.g., `3600s`).
151	
152	//axhrgb_group
153	{rbrr_group_public_docs}
154	
155	Public documentation URL for user-facing references.
156	
157	//axhrgv_variable
158	{rbrr_public_docs_url}
159	
160	Complete URL for the public documentation entry point.
161	Updated per-release (swap branch name) or per-incorporation (point at a fork).
162	Used by buh_tlt glossary links and user-facing output.
163	
164	//axhro_kindle
165	{rbkro_kindle}
166	
167	Kindles the {rbrr_regime} fields by enrolling each field with its type-specific constraints under named groups.
168	Detects unexpected RBRR_ variables.
169	Composes the docker environment injection set and locks the regime against further modification.
170	
171	//axhro_validate
172	{rbkro_validate}
173	
174	Validates enrolled fields against type-specific constraints from enrollment.
175	Validates directory existence for {rbrr_vessel_dir} and {rbrr_secrets_dir}.
176	Enforces format constraints on {rbrd_cloud_prefix} and {rbrd_depot_moniker}, and the joint-length cap on `${RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER}` (≤30 chars per the GCP project ID limit).
177	
178	//axhro_render
179	{rbkro_render}
180	
181	Renders the {rbrr_regime} grouped by section with presentation gates per group.
182	Sections: Resource Prefixing, Vessel and Local Configuration, GCP Infrastructure, Google Cloud Build Configuration, Secrets Directory, Public Docs.

//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_string
{rbrd_gcb_machine_type}::
Compute Engine machine type for the private worker pool, consumed at pool creation time by
{rbtgo_depot_levy}.
Default: `e2-standard-2`.
The pool resource holds this value; individual build requests do not specify machine type.

NOTE: *Private pool is always required.*
Private pools are a prerequisite for VPC Service Controls and `NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS` hardening (see `RBSCB-CloudBuildPosture.adoc`).
Pricing is region-dependent: in us-central1/us-east4, private pool e2-standard-2 matches the default pool rate ($0.006/build-minute); in other regions the private pool rate is up to 1.4× higher.
This cost is accepted because the security prerequisite is non-negotiable and maintaining two code paths (conditional pool vs. default) doubles configuration surface.
+
Cloud Build supports two distinct machine type naming systems:
the default shared pool uses Cloud Build enum values (`E2_HIGHCPU_8`, `N1_HIGHCPU_32`) in `options.machineType` of the build JSON;
private pools use Compute Engine instance type names (`e2-standard-2`, `n2d-standard-4`, `c3-standard-8`) in `workerConfig.machineType` at pool creation.
Valid private pool families: `e2`, `n2d`, `c3` (availability varies by region).
Since Recipe Bottle uses only private pools, only the Compute Engine format is valid for
{rbrd_gcb_machine_type}.

[[rbrr_group_cbv2]]
//axvr_group
{rbrr_group_cbv2}::
include::RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc[]

// RBRG regime eliminated — reliquaries replace pin-based tool image management
// Tool images now sourced from datestamped reliquary namespace in GAR at ordain time

=== Depot Regime (RBRD)

The
regime
defines depot-time-immutable identity and infrastructure settings frozen at depot levy.
Changing any field after levy requires a fresh depot.

[[rbrd_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton
{rbrd_regime}::
Depot-time-immutable identity and infrastructure settings shared by all operations targeting a single depot.

[[rbrd_cloud_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{rbrd_cloud_prefix}::
Prefix prepended to all cloud-visible resource names under the depot: GAR image paths, vessel package names,
{gcb_service}
substitutions, and other GCP-scoped identifiers.
Must be 2–11 characters, start with a lowercase letter, and end with a hyphen.

[[rbrd_depot_moniker]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{rbrd_depot_moniker}::
Stem identifier that combines with
{rbrd_cloud_prefix}
to form the depot identity tuple.
Must be 1–26 characters, start with a lowercase letter, and contain only lowercase alphanumerics.
The tuple derives `RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM`, and `RBDC_GCS_BUCKET` at kindle time.

[[rbrd_gcp_region]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtg_region
{rbrd_gcp_region}::
Default
{gcp_service}
region for regional services like
{gar_service}
and
{gcb_service}.

[[rbrd_gcb_machine_type]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_string
{rbrd_gcb_machine_type}::
Compute Engine machine type for the private worker pool, consumed at pool creation time by
{rbtgo_depot_levy}.
Default: `e2-standard-2`.
The pool resource holds this value; individual build requests do not specify machine type.

NOTE: *Private pool is always required.*
Private pools are a prerequisite for VPC Service Controls and `NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS` hardening (see `RBSCB-CloudBuildPosture.adoc`).
Pricing is region-dependent: in us-central1/us-east4, private pool e2-standard-2 matches the default pool rate ($0.006/build-minute); in other regions the private pool rate is up to 1.4× higher.
This cost is accepted because the security prerequisite is non-negotiable and maintaining two code paths (conditional pool vs. default) doubles configuration surface.
+
Cloud Build supports two distinct machine type naming systems:
the default shared pool uses Cloud Build enum values (`E2_HIGHCPU_8`, `N1_HIGHCPU_32`) in `options.machineType` of the build JSON;
private pools use Compute Engine instance type names (`e2-standard-2`, `n2d-standard-4`, `c3-standard-8`) in `workerConfig.machineType` at pool creation.
Valid private pool families: `e2`, `n2d`, `c3` (availability varies by region).
Since Recipe Bottle uses only private pools, only the Compute Engine format is valid for
{rbrd_gcb_machine_type}.

[[rbrd_group_depot_identity]]
//axvr_group
{rbrd_group_depot_identity}::
Cloud-namespace identity tuple consumed by
{rbtgo_depot_levy}
to derive every depot-scoped GCP resource name.

[[rbrd_group_region]]
//axvr_group
{rbrd_group_region}::
GCP region applied to depot-scoped regional services.

[[rbrd_group_pool]]
//axvr_group
{rbrd_group_pool}::
Cloud Build private worker pool configuration consumed at pool creation time.

include::RBSRT-RegimeDepot.adoc[]

=== Recipe Bottle GCP Constants (RBGC)

file.content:
zbuv_kindle
  buv_dir_exists  "${RBV_TEMP_DIR}"
  buv_dir_empty   "${RBV_TEMP_DIR}"
  buv_file_exists "${RBV_RBRR_FILE}"
  buv_file_exists "${RBV_RBRS_FILE}"

  # Source config files (CLI handles all sourcing)
  source              "${RBV_RBRR_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRR config"
  source              ".rbk/rbrm.env"    || buc_die "Failed to source RBRM config"
  zrbrr_kindle
  zrbrd_kindle
  zrbrr_enforce
  zrbrd_enforce
  zrbrm_kindle
  zrbrm_enforce
  zrbdc_kindle

  source              "${RBV_RBRS_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRS config"
  zrbrs_kindle
  zrbrs_enforce

17	#
18	# Recipe Bottle VM - Command Line Interface
19	
20	set -euo pipefail
21	
22	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
23	
24	# CLI-specific environment function
25	zrbv_furnish() {
26	  # Handle documentation mode
27	  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
28	  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
29	  buc_doc_env "RBV_TEMP_DIR          " "Empty temporary directory"
30	  buc_doc_env "RBV_RBRR_FILE         " "File containing the RBRR constants"
31	  buc_doc_env "RBV_RBRS_FILE         " "File containing the RBRS constants"
32	  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
33	
34	  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
35	  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"
36	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
37	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
38	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrd_regime.sh"
39	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrm_regime.sh"
40	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
41	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrs_regime.sh"
42	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbv_PodmanVM.sh"
43	
44	  # Initialize enrollment and validate environment
45	  zbuv_kindle
46	  buv_dir_exists  "${RBV_TEMP_DIR}"
47	  buv_dir_empty   "${RBV_TEMP_DIR}"
48	  buv_file_exists "${RBV_RBRR_FILE}"
49	  buv_file_exists "${RBV_RBRS_FILE}"

#!/bin/bash
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle VM - Command Line Interface

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

# CLI-specific environment function
zrbv_furnish() {
  # Handle documentation mode
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "RBV_TEMP_DIR          " "Empty temporary directory"
  buc_doc_env "RBV_RBRR_FILE         " "File containing the RBRR constants"
  buc_doc_env "RBV_RBRS_FILE         " "File containing the RBRS constants"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrd_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrm_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrs_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbv_PodmanVM.sh"

  # Initialize enrollment and validate environment
  zbuv_kindle
  buv_dir_exists  "${RBV_TEMP_DIR}"
  buv_dir_empty   "${RBV_TEMP_DIR}"
  buv_file_exists "${RBV_RBRR_FILE}"
  buv_file_exists "${RBV_RBRS_FILE}"

  # Source config files (CLI handles all sourcing)
  source              "${RBV_RBRR_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRR config"
  source              ".rbk/rbrm.env"    || buc_die "Failed to source RBRM config"
  zrbrr_kindle
  zrbrd_kindle
  zrbrr_enforce
  zrbrd_enforce
  zrbrm_kindle
  zrbrm_enforce
  zrbdc_kindle

  source              "${RBV_RBRS_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRS config"
  zrbrs_kindle
  zrbrs_enforce

  # Start implementation module
  zrbv_kindle
}

# Execute command
buc_execute rbv_ "Recipe Bottle VM - Podman Virtual Machine Management" zrbv_furnish "$@"

# eof

content.content: 47:All configuration flows through [Regimes](#Regime) — structured `.env` files with typed validation, each with its own render and validate commands. 48:Some regimes are committed in the repo: [Vessel](#Vessel) definitions ([RBRV](#RBRV)), [Nameplate](#Nameplate) configurations ([RBRN](#RBRN)), [Depot](#Depot) identity ([RBRR](#RBRR)), and [Payor](#Payor) identity ([RBRP](#RBRP)). 138:[Hallmark](#Hallmark) values are recorded into [Nameplate](#Nameplate) [Regime](#Regime) files to pin a [Nameplate](#Nameplate) to specific image versions. 173:[Hallmark](#Hallmark) values from the [Tally](#Tally) are recorded into [Nameplate](#Nameplate) [Regime](#Regime) files, completing the chain from build to runtime. 268:This is a [Payor](#Payor) operation that binds [Regime](#Regime) configuration to real cloud resources. 490:- **Credential confinement** - Move cloud service credentials (API keys, IAM keys, SSH keys) from the operator's workstation into the [Crucible](#Crucible), injected via [Nameplate](#Nameplate) [Regime](#Regime) configuration. 529:| `├── .buk/` | [BURC](#BURC) project structure [Regime](#Regime) | 530:| `├── .rbk/` | [Regime](#Regime) configuration root | 532:| `│ ├── rbrr.env` | [RBRR](#RBRR) — [Depot](#Depot) identity and build configuration | 575:## Appendix: Specific Regimes 584:**[RBRR](#RBRR)** — [Depot](#Depot) identity and build configuration — populated during [Levy](#Levy), consumed by [Director](#Director) and [Retriever](#Retriever) operations.

` ├── common-ifrit-context/` | Shared [Ifrit](#Ifrit) build context (tether + airgap variants) | 556 | ` │ ├── Dockerfile.tether` | Rust binary + network attack tools (tether build) | 557 | ` │ ├── Dockerfile.airgap` | Same image built `FROM` the forge ([Airgap](#Airgap) build, no upstream pull) | 558 | ` │ ├── Cargo.toml` | Ifrit crate manifest | 559 | ` │ └── src/` | Ifrit source (rbid binary) | 560 | ` ├── common-ifrit-forge-context/` | Build context for the [Ifrit](#Ifrit) forge — warms cargo cache + pre-stages apt deps | 561 | ` │ └── Dockerfile` | Forge image; consumed by `Dockerfile.airgap` as its base | 562 | ` ├── rbev-bottle-ifrit-tether/` | [Conjure](#Conjure) (tether) — [Ifrit](#Ifrit) attack binary | 563 | ` │ └── rbrv.env` | [RBRV](#RBRV) — [Conjure](#Conjure) mode, tether egress | 564 | ` ├── rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap/` | [Conjure](#Conjure) ([Airgap](#Airgap)) — [Ifrit](#Ifrit) attack binary, [Ordained](#Ordain) without upstream pull | 565 | ` │ └── rbrv.env` | [RBRV](#RBRV) — [Conjure](#Conjure) mode, airgap egress | 566 | ` ├── rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge/` | [Conjure](#Conjure) (tether) — Build-time forge fixture for [Airgap](#Airgap) [Ifrit](#Ifrit) | 567 | ` │ └── rbrv.env` | [RBRV](#RBRV) — [Conjure](#Conjure) mode, tether egress | 568 | ` ├── rbev-bottle-plantuml/` | [Bind](#Bind) — upstream image pinned by digest | 569 | ` │ └── rbrv.env` | [RBRV](#RBRV) — [Bind](#Bind) mode, digest reference | 570 | ` ├── rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter/` | [Conjure](#Conjure) — Jupyter notebook server | 571 | ` │ ├── Dockerfile` | | 572 | ` │ └── rbrv.env` | [RBRV](#RBRV) — [Conjure](#Conjure) mode | 573 | ` └── (4 additional test vessels)` | busybox variants for [Theurge](#Theurge) fixture coverage | 574 575 ## Appendix: Specific Regimes 576 577 **[BURC](#BURC)** — Project structure configuration, in the repo. 578 [Tabtarget](#Tabtarget) directory, tools directory. 579 580 **[BURS](#BURS)** — Developer workstation configuration. 581 Not in git. 582 Log directory, station paths. 583 584 **[RBRR](#RBRR)** — [Depot](#Depot) identity and build configuration — populated during [Levy](#Levy), consumed by [Director](#Director) and [Retriever](#Retriever) operations. 585 586 **[RBRP](#RBRP)** — [Manor](#Manor) identity — billing account, OAuth client ID, operator email, and the [Manor's](#Manor) GCP project. 587 In the repo. 588 589 **[RBRO](#RBRO)** — [Payor](#Payor) OAuth credentials — client secret and refresh token. 590 Not in the repo. 591 592 **[RBRA](#RBRA)** — Role credentials resident on user workstation, enabling [Governor](#Governor), [Director](#Director), or [Retriever](#Retriever) operations. 593 One credential file per role per [Depot](#Depot). 594

` │ └── rbrv.env` | [RBRV](#RBRV) — [Bind](#Bind) mode, digest reference | | ` ├── rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter/` | [Conjure](#Conjure) — Jupyter notebook server | | ` │ ├── Dockerfile` | | | ` │ └── rbrv.env` | [RBRV](#RBRV) — [Conjure](#Conjure) mode | | ` └── (4 additional test vessels)` | busybox variants for [Theurge](#Theurge) fixture coverage | ## Appendix: Specific Regimes **[BURC](#BURC)** — Project structure configuration, in the repo. [Tabtarget](#Tabtarget) directory, tools directory. **[BURS](#BURS)** — Developer workstation configuration. Not in git. Log directory, station paths. **[RBRR](#RBRR)** — [Depot](#Depot) identity and build configuration — populated during [Levy](#Levy), consumed by [Director](#Director) and [Retriever](#Retriever) operations. **[RBRP](#RBRP)** — [Manor](#Manor) identity — billing account, OAuth client ID, operator email, and the [Manor's](#Manor) GCP project. In the repo. **[RBRO](#RBRO)** — [Payor](#Payor) OAuth credentials — client secret and refresh token. Not in the repo. **[RBRA](#RBRA)** — Role credentials resident on user workstation, enabling [Governor](#Governor), [Director](#Director), or [Retriever](#Retriever) operations. One credential file per role per [Depot](#Depot).

+Some regimes are committed in the repo: [Vessel](#Vessel) definitions ([RBRV](#RBRV)), [Nameplate](#Nameplate) configurations ([RBRN](#RBRN)), [Depot](#Depot) identity ([RBRD](#RBRD)), repository-wide settings ([RBRR](#RBRR)), and [Payor](#Payor) identity ([RBRP](#RBRP)).
Others live on the filesystem outside revision control: OAuth credentials ([RBRO](#RBRO)), role credentials ([RBRA](#RBRA)), and developer workstation paths ([BURS](#BURS)).
Every operation is launched through a [Tabtarget](#Tabtarget) — a shell script in the `tt/` directory.
